content.full_text:
January 5, 1978


Mr. Robert V. Arellancs
Ferrales Campaign Committee
2317 Pintura Way
Rancho Cordova, California 95670

Dear Mr. Arellancs:

Your letter dated November 21, 1977 addressed to the California
Secretary of State, March Fong Eu, has been referred to this
office for reply.

You have inquired whether it is necessary for you, as treasurer
of the Ferrales Campaign Committee, to obtain receipts and invoices
from the candidate before reimbursing the candidate for campaign
related expenditures he/she incurred such as the purchase of gaso-
line and meals.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has adopted an administra-
tive regulation (2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18401) which prescribes
required recordkeeping for persons governed by the campaign disclo-
sure provisions, Chapter 4 of the Political Reform Act. The regula-
tion states: "It shall be the duty of each candidate, treasurer
and elected officer to keep such detailed accounts, records, bills
and receipts as shall be reasonably necessary to comply with the
provisions of Chapter 4, Government Code Section 84100, at seq."
(Emphasis added.)

This regulation implies that original supporting documents such as
receipts and invoices should be maintained for all expenditures,
except those where it is impossible or impractical to obtain them.

Where original supporting documents cannot be obtained, it is re-
commended that a written voucher be prepared by the person request-
ing reimbursement indicating when the expenditure were incurred,
where it was incurred, the amount of the expenditure and a descrip-
tion of the transaction.

Therefore, it is not necessary for you to obtain original supporting
documents from the candidate for every expenditure he or she has
incurred and is seeking reimbursement. However, it is recommended
that a written voucher be obtained from the candidate for those
expenditures that are not supported by original supporting documents
such as invoices and receipts.

If I can be of further assistance, do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,


ALAN HERNDON
Special Compliance Representative


AH:bw

Office of the Secretary of State
March Fong Eu

111 Capitol Mall, Rm. 200
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95867

POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
(916) 322-4880

December 8, 1977

Mr. Robert V. Arellanes
Ferrales Campaign Committee
I.D. Number 771082
2317 Pintura Way
Rancho Cordova, CA 95670

Dear Mr. Arellanes:

Thank you for your letter of November 21, 1977, regarding reimbursement of candidate expenses by your committee.

I can find no one in the Political Reform Division who recalls
a conversation with you. It appears that the question you
pose should be answered by the staff of the Fair Political
Practices Commission (FPPC). One of their functions is to
explain the duties of persons and committees under the
Political Reform Act of 1974.

I am referring a copy of your letter to the FPPC for review.
Should you have any additional questions of this nature, you
may contact the Commission's Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division by calling 322-5660.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform

GENE R. MORROW, Auditor
Political Reform Division

cc: FPPC/

Office of the Secretary of State
March Fong Eu
Political Reform Division
111 Capitol Mall, Rm. 200
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

Monday
November 21, 1977

To Whom It May Concern:

This morning I discussed by telephone the following
item with a representative of your office, and requested written
confirmation of the information passed on over the telephone.
Essentially what the matter boils down to is that the
candidate incurred expenses for gasoline and attendance at
a variety of functions requiring the purchase of a ticket,
meal, and occasional refreshments. The problem is that the
candidate does not have receipts for all of the expenditures
of the type listed above.

I am prepared to reimburse the candidate and report
that the expenses were incurred in the course of and towards
the campaign itself. Before doing so, however, a letter
verifying the information passed on over the telephone, to
wit, that expenditures on gasoline and function attendance are
valid and that receipts, though helpful, are not required.

Thanking you in advance for your attendance.

Yours truly,

Robert V. Arellanes
Robert V. Arellanes
Treasurer,
Ferrales Campaign Committee
771082 I D
2317 Pinturo Way
Rancho Cordova, Calif. 95670

RECEIVED
AND FILED
in the Office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State
